name: @vibe-engineer-starter/golden-web-fixture
description: I-17A golden-web fixture: the RENDERABLE golden web app (Vite + React) derived from the I-15B .source-template/apps/web pattern, wired to consume the I-16B shared client (golden-client useGoldenRecords accessor + web transport → I-16A provider) so the golden-records route renders REAL classified records (DL-16 §245/§247). Includes a deterministic served-app wiring harness (dev-server spawn + readiness probe + teardown). Domain-neutral sample/demo/reference (DL-14 / DL-16 / DL-20A). No contract/client re-declaration (DL-14 §5).

vibeEngineer.consumptionModel: consumes I-16B shared client (golden-client) + I-16A provider via the public fixture seam only (no duplicated contract/client/DTO source)
vibeEngineer.boundaryRule: golden-web derives ONLY from the I-15B source-template pattern + the I-16B shared client; renders via the web transport against the real I-16A provider (DL-14 §5; DL-16 §247)

//dependency-model: Graph-neutral: this fixture path is NOT a pnpm workspace member (pnpm-workspace.yaml = packages/*, packages/presets/*, packages/adapters/*, infra/pulumi), so the modeled runtime deps below add ZERO edges to the real harness workspace graph and require no lockfile reconciliation. The REAL resolve of react/react-dom/vite/@playwright/test requires a serialized EXTEND dep handoff (root manifest + pnpm-lock.yaml reconcile, I-20S-class) — they are ABSENT from the entire repo (verified: 0 in lockfile, none in node_modules, no resolve-hook anchor context exists, contrast I-16B which anchored @ts-rest/core+zod at packages/contracts). The I-16B shared-client transitive deps (@ts-rest/core + zod) are bound into the served bundle via a Vite resolve.alias in vite.config.ts that anchors at the harness packages/contracts context (the same real-resolution posture as golden-flow/src/runtime/dep-resolver-hooks.mjs). NO install, NO manifest/lockfile edit, NO mock is performed in-license.
